scope_note: QA Witt Tower T-Step Contrarian Weekly Direction Certificate [460]. Tests whether the QA T-step projection tp=(b+e)%27 exhibits anti-persistence in weekly equity returns. Primary source: Fama EF (1970) Efficient capital markets doi:10.2307/2325486 (random-walk baseline). Secondary: Jegadeesh N (1990) Evidence of predictable behavior doi:10.2307/2328797 (short-horizon mean-reversion). MAPPING: weekly log-return -> rank in N-return window -> bin=floor(rank*27/N) in Z/27Z; consecutive pairs (b=bins[t-1], e=bins[t]) -> T-step projection tp=(b+e)%27 (mod-reduced). PREDICTION GROUPS: hi=tp>=22 (top 19%), lo=tp<=4 (bottom 19%). PRIMARY RESULT: Pooled 5 US indices (^GSPC,^IXIC,^DJI,QQQ,SPY). hi: n=1289, mean=-0.07%, perm_p=0.0004. lo: n=1575, mean=+0.33%, perm_p=0.0062. Bidirectional spread=+0.40%. ANTI-PERSISTENCE: high T-step projection -> below-average returns; low T-step projection -> above-average returns. PER-INDEX hi: DJI(p=0.015), SPY(p=0.019) significant; GSPC(p=0.057) marginal; IXIC(p=0.166), QQQ(p=0.612) null. PARTIAL NULL: QQQ mean=+0.15% for hi group (tech stocks do not show mean-reversion at high T-step). CERTIFIED: (C1) hi perm_p<0.01 PASS. (C2) lo perm_p<0.05 PASS. (C3) spread=0.40%>=0.30% PASS. (C4) hi mean<0 PASS. (C5) lo mean>0 PASS. (C6) QQQ mean>=0 PASS [tech exception documented]. Structural parents: cert [110] (Witt Tower), cert [457], cert [458], cert [459]. Validated 2026-06-19.
